component.description: Autoswitching Power Mux with low rDS(on) switches and adjustable current limit

absolute_maximum_ratings.raw_name: Input voltage range at pins IN1, IN2, EN, VSNS, ILIM
absolute_maximum_ratings.conditions: All voltages are with respect to GND

absolute_maximum_ratings.raw_name: Lead temperature soldering 1,6 mm (1/16 inch) from case for 10 seconds

electrical_characteristics.conditions: VI(VSNS) = 1.5 V, EN = Low (IN1 active), VI(IN1) = 5.5 V, VI(IN2) = 3.3 V, IO(OUT) = 0 A
EN = High (inactive), VI(IN1) = 5.5 V, VI(IN2) = 3.3 V, IO(OUT) = 0 A

pin_definitions.description: STAT is an open-drain output that is Hi-Z if the IN2 switch is ON. STAT pulls low if the IN1 switch is ON or if OUT is Hi-Z (i.e., EN is equal to logic 0)
EN is a TTL and CMOS compatible input with a 1-µA pull-up. The truth table shown above illustrates the functionality of EN.
An internal power FET connects OUT to IN1 if the VSNS voltage is greater than 0.8 V. Otherwise, the FET connects OUT to the higher of IN1 and IN2.
A resistor RILIM from ILIM to GND sets the current limit IL to 250/RILIM and 500/RILIM for the TPS2112 and TPS2113, respectively.
Secondary power switch input. The IN2 switch can be enabled only if the IN2 supply is above the UVLO threshold and at least one supply exceeds the internal VDD UVLO.
Primary power switch input. The IN1 switch can be enabled only if the IN1 supply is above the UVLO threshold and at least one supply exceeds the internal VDD UVLO.

logical_pins.name: STAT
logical_pins.description: Open-drain status output. STAT is Hi-Z if the IN2 switch is ON. STAT pulls low if the IN1 switch is ON or if OUT is Hi-Z (i.e., EN is high).

logical_pins.name: EN
logical_pins.description: Active-low enable input. TTL and CMOS compatible with an internal 1-µA pull-up.

logical_pins.name: VSNS
logical_pins.description: Voltage sense input. Connects OUT to IN1 if VSNS > 0.8 V; otherwise, OUT connects to the higher of IN1 and IN2.

logical_pins.name: ILIM
logical_pins.description: Current limit setting pin. A resistor from ILIM to GND sets the current limit.

1.name: STAT
1.description: Open-drain status output. STAT is Hi-Z if the IN2 switch is ON. STAT pulls low if the IN1 switch is ON or if OUT is Hi-Z (i.e., EN is high).

2.name: EN
2.description: Active-low enable input. TTL and CMOS compatible with an internal 1-µA pull-up.

3.name: VSNS
3.description: Voltage sense input. Connects OUT to IN1 if VSNS > 0.8 V; otherwise, OUT connects to the higher of IN1 and IN2.

4.name: ILIM
4.description: Current limit setting pin. A resistor from ILIM to GND sets the current limit.
